chunks.title: CSS Animations and Transitions
chunks.text: Welcome back. Today we will learn about CSS animations and transitions.

chunks.title: CSS Animations and Transitions
chunks.text: CSS transitions allow you to change property values smoothly over a given duration.

chunks.title: CSS Animations and Transitions
chunks.text: The transition property is a shorthand for transition-property, transition-duration, transition-timing-function, and transition-delay.

chunks.title: CSS Animations and Transitions
chunks.text: For example, transition all 0.3s ease will animate all property changes over 0.3 seconds.

chunks.title: CSS Animations and Transitions
chunks.text: The ease timing function starts slow, speeds up, then slows down at the end.

chunks.title: CSS Animations and Transitions
chunks.text: Linear timing function has the same speed from start to end.

chunks.title: CSS Animations and Transitions
chunks.text: Ease-in starts slow and speeds up. Ease-out starts fast and slows down.

chunks.title: CSS Animations and Transitions
chunks.text: CSS animations are more powerful than transitions as they allow multiple keyframes.

chunks.title: CSS Animations and Transitions
chunks.text: You define animations using @keyframes rule with a name.

chunks.title: CSS Animations and Transitions
chunks.text: Inside keyframes, you define what styles the element should have at certain points.

chunks.title: CSS Animations and Transitions
chunks.text: You can use from and to, or percentage values like 0%, 25%, 50%, 75%, 100%.

chunks.title: CSS Animations and Transitions
chunks.text: The animation property is a shorthand for animation-name, duration, timing-function, delay, iteration-count, and direction.

chunks.title: CSS Animations and Transitions
chunks.text: Animation-iteration-count can be set to infinite for continuous animations.

chunks.title: CSS Animations and Transitions
chunks.text: Animation-direction alternate makes the animation play forwards then backwards.

chunks.title: CSS Animations and Transitions
chunks.text: The transform property is often used with animations for smooth visual effects.

chunks.title: CSS Animations and Transitions
chunks.text: Transform translate moves an element, rotate rotates it, scale changes its size.

chunks.title: CSS Animations and Transitions
chunks.text: Transform does not affect the document flow, so other elements are not pushed around.

chunks.title: CSS Animations and Transitions
chunks.text: This makes transform ideal for animations as it performs well in browsers.

chunks.title: CSS Animations and Transitions
chunks.text: Let me show you how to create a loading spinner animation using pure CSS.

chunks.title: CSS Animations and Transitions
chunks.text: We will also create a hover effect that scales and rotates a card element.

chunks.title: CSS Animations and Transitions
chunks.text: CSS animations can replace many JavaScript animations and perform better.

chunks.title: CSS Animations and Transitions
chunks.text: Always prefer CSS animations over JavaScript animations when possible for better performance.

chunks.title: CSS Animations and Transitions
chunks.text: The will-change property can hint to the browser about upcoming animations for optimization.

chunks.title: CSS Animations and Transitions
chunks.text: In the next video, we will learn about some more advanced CSS concepts.

text: Welcome back. Today we will learn about CSS animations and transitions. CSS transitions allow you to change property values smoothly over a given duration. The transition property is a shorthand for transition-property, transition-duration, transition-timing-function, and transition-delay. For example, transition all 0.3s ease will animate all property changes over 0.3 seconds. The ease timing function starts slow, speeds up, then slows down at the end. Linear timing function has the same speed from start to end. Ease-in starts slow and speeds up. Ease-out starts fast and slows down. CSS animations are more powerful than transitions as they allow multiple keyframes. You define animations using @keyframes rule with a name. Inside keyframes, you define what styles the element should have at certain points. You can use from and to, or percentage values like 0%, 25%, 50%, 75%, 100%. The animation property is a shorthand for animation-name, duration, timing-function, delay, iteration-count, and direction. Animation-iteration-count can be set to infinite for continuous animations. Animation-direction alternate makes the animation play forwards then backwards. The transform property is often used with animations for smooth visual effects. Transform translate moves an element, rotate rotates it, scale changes its size. Transform does not affect the document flow, so other elements are not pushed around. This makes transform ideal for animations as it performs well in browsers. Let me show you how to create a loading spinner animation using pure CSS. We will also create a hover effect that scales and rotates a card element. CSS animations can replace many JavaScript animations and perform better. Always prefer CSS animations over JavaScript animations when possible for better performance. The will-change property can hint to the browser about upcoming animations for optimization. In the next video, we will learn about some more advanced CSS concepts.
